details.body: Wire planned-elsewhere findings through the actual final-review/reconciliation path instead of only adding a standalone helper. `src/contexts/workflow_composition/panel_contracts.rs:137-154` still only allows free-form amendments, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1523-1553` still marks any accepted amendment as `restart_required`, and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7359-7378` still turns every accepted final-review item into a `QueuedAmendment`. The new handler in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:81-158` is not invoked from that flow, so there is currently no way to carry `mapped_to_bead_id` through review or let the active bead complete without a reopen/restart loop.

Make `record_planned_elsewhere_mapping` transactional across `planned_elsewhere.ndjson` and the milestone journal. In `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2352-2380` the mapping file is appended first and the `PlannedElsewhereMapped` journal event is appended second, with no rollback if `append_event` fails. A journal write error will therefore leave a persisted mapping with no audit event.

Tighten stale-bead fallback so only definitive missing/closed bead results are downgraded to warnings. In `src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:55-73`, any `br show` failure is treated as `bead_verified = false` and reconciliation proceeds. Timeouts, parse failures, or repo/configuration errors would therefore be misclassified as stale-bead cases instead of surfacing an operator-visible failure.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': Claude returned empty result with no structured_output (contract: plan_and_implement, stdout_len: 1176, stderr_len: 0, session_policy: ReuseIfAllowed, stdout_tail: "2256,\"cacheReadInputTokens\":12187320,\"cacheCreationInputTokens\":243797,\"webSearchRequests\":0,\"costUSD\":8.690006249999998,\"contextWindow\":200000,\"maxOutputTokens\":64000},\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\":{\"inputTokens\":78,\"outputTokens\":7789,\"cacheReadInputTokens\":473270,\"cacheCreationInputTokens\":35272,\"webSearchRequests\":0,\"costUSD\":0.13044,\"contextWindow\":200000,\"maxOutputTokens\":32000}},\"permission_denials\":[],\"terminal_reason\":\"completed\",\"fast_mode_state\":\"off\",\"uuid\":\"ed5c20c7-fbf8-4e3f-980a-1aa624f4c87d\"}\n", stderr_tail: "")

details.body: In `record_planned_elsewhere_amendments` (engine.rs:7538), the `mapped_bead_verified` field is hardcoded to `false` with a comment saying verification is deferred to `automation_runtime`. However, the `automation_runtime::planned_elsewhere::reconcile_planned_elsewhere` function — which does perform verification — is never called from the engine or from `success_reconciliation`. The engine records unverified mappings, but there is no code path that later invokes the verification handler to update them. Either the automation_runtime reconciler should be wired into the success_reconciliation flow to verify pending unverified mappings, or the engine should call `reconcile_planned_elsewhere` directly instead of `record_planned_elsewhere_amendments`. As it stands, `mapped_bead_verified` will always be `false` for mappings created through the engine path.

There are no tests for the `mapped_to_bead_id: Some(...)` path in the final_review aggregation logic. The `has_non_planned_elsewhere` flag, the partitioning of amendments into planned-elsewhere vs regular in the engine, and the no-restart behavior when all amendments are planned-elsewhere are all untested. The existing final_review tests only set `mapped_to_bead_id: None`. At minimum, add a test for `aggregate_final_review_panel` where some amendments have `mapped_to_bead_id: Some("...")` to verify that `restart_required` is false when all accepted amendments are planned-elsewhere.

The shipped execution path never performs the stale-bead lookup or optional `br comment` that this feature is supposed to add. The final-review engine records planned-elsewhere items by calling `record_planned_elsewhere_amendments` from `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2655-2668` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2864-2878`, and that helper hardcodes `mapped_bead_verified: false` before writing the mapping in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7607-7613`. The only code that actually verifies the mapped bead and posts comments lives in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:103-157`, but this branch does not route the production final-review flow through that handler. As a result, stale bead references are never detected in production and the optional `br comment` path is unreachable.

Planned-elsewhere mappings are persisted before the final-review stage commit is known to have succeeded, and they are not rolled back if the stage later fails. In the completion path, mappings are written at `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2655-2668` before the stage-completed journal append at `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2670-2698`. In the restart path, mappings are written at `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2864-2878` before queue writes and later failure handling at `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2880-2975`. Because `record_planned_elsewhere_mapping` appends directly to the milestone journal and `planned_elsewhere.ndjson` in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2353-2390`, any later stage failure leaves behind durable mapping state for a final-review attempt that never committed, and retries will append duplicates.

Final-review deduplication collapses amendments solely by normalized body and silently keeps the first non-`None` mapping target, so conflicting ownership classifications can be misrouted. `merge_final_review_amendments` in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:154-183` uses only `normalized_body` as the dedup key and, on collision, preserves whatever `mapped_to_bead_id` it saw first. Later, restart behavior is decided purely from that surviving field in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1533-1591`. If two reviewers submit the same amendment text but disagree on whether it is planned-elsewhere, or disagree on which bead owns it, one reviewer's classification is discarded based on proposal order and the run can incorrectly skip a restart or record the mapping against the wrong bead.

verify_and_comment_mappings (planned_elsewhere.rs:247) and verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success (success_reconciliation.rs:674) compute verification outcomes but never persist the updated mapped_bead_verified=true back to the NDJSON file. This means every subsequent call to verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success will re-process the same mappings (re-verifying via br show and re-posting the same br comment on the mapped-to bead). Fix: after verify_and_comment_mappings returns, update the NDJSON entries for mappings whose verified field changed to true, or adopt an append-only design where a verification event supersedes the original unverified record.

In final_review.rs:1503, the force-completed early return checks `!final_accepted_amendments.is_empty() && final_review_restart_count >= max_restarts` without distinguishing planned-elsewhere amendments. When ALL accepted amendments are planned-elsewhere and restart_count >= max_restarts (from prior rounds), this path triggers unnecessarily, setting force_completed=true and emitting a misleading 'restart cap reached' summary. The check should be `final_accepted_amendments.iter().any(|a| a.mapped_to_bead_id.is_none()) && final_review_restart_count >= max_restarts` to only force-complete when there are actual regular amendments that would require a restart.

After posting a planned-elsewhere `br comment`, run `br sync --flush-only` (or batch comments before a final sync) so the comment mutation is durable. Reconciliation syncs at `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:200-209` and only then posts comments via `src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:285-313`; `comment_bead` marks the adapter dirty until `sync_flush` runs (`src/adapters/br_process.rs:510-512`, `src/adapters/br_process.rs:607-617`), so successful comments can be lost on exit.

Persist post-run verification results back into milestone state/journal. The engine records every mapping as `mapped_bead_verified: false` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7613-7618`), and reconciliation filters on that flag (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:699-718`), but `verify_and_comment_mappings` only returns outcomes and never updates `planned_elsewhere.ndjson` or the journal (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:247-323`). As written, a verified mapping stays permanently 'unverified', so any reconciliation replay will re-verify and re-comment the same bead.

Make the journal and `planned_elsewhere.ndjson` writes recoverable/atomic. `record_planned_elsewhere_mapping` writes the journal first and says the sidecar can be rebuilt from it (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2348-2390`), but `load_planned_elsewhere_mappings` only reads the sidecar (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2395-2401`) and the engine merely logs and continues on sidecar-write failure (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7621-7657`). A crash or I/O error after the journal append leaves a mapping present in audit history but invisible to later reload/comment processing (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:684-702`).

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': schema validation failed for stage 'plan_and_implement': missing field `change_summary`

details.body: Add tests for rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal (service.rs:2418) covering: (1) successful rebuild from journal events when NDJSON sidecar is missing, (2) correct extraction of all PlannedElsewhereMapping fields from journal event metadata, (3) graceful handling of journal events with missing metadata fields (active_bead_id, mapped_to_bead_id). Also add a test for the verified-mapping re-persistence loop in verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success (success_reconciliation.rs:744-771).

src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:744-756 and src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2400-2413: post-success verification appends a second `PlannedElsewhereMapping` row with `mapped_bead_verified=true` and assumes "the latest record for a given mapping wins", but `load_planned_elsewhere_mappings()` just returns every NDJSON row verbatim. After a restart, the original `mapped_bead_verified=false` row is still loaded, so the same finding is re-verified and can post duplicate `br comment`s on every replay. Collapse mappings by identity when loading (for example by `(active_bead_id, finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id)` keeping the newest row), or update the existing record instead of append-only writes.

src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2385-2390 and src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2400-2413: the recovery path does not actually honor the journal-first durability model. If `append_event()` succeeds but `append_mapping()` fails after `planned_elsewhere.ndjson` already contains older rows, `load_planned_elsewhere_mappings()` returns the non-empty sidecar and never rebuilds from the authoritative journal, so the newly journaled mapping disappears after restart. The loader needs to merge/reconcile journal events with the sidecar, or rebuild current state from the journal whenever they can diverge.

In `record_planned_elsewhere_mapping` (service.rs:2353), the docstring says 'If the secondary NDJSON write fails, the journal still contains the mapping for audit purposes' but the function propagates the NDJSON error via `?` at line 2390. This means callers see `Err` even though the authoritative journal record was successfully written. Either (a) downgrade the NDJSON failure to a warning log and return `Ok`, consistent with the stated 'secondary' semantics, or (b) update the docstring to say the function returns an error on NDJSON failure despite the journal succeeding. Currently all callers handle the error gracefully (best-effort paths), so this is a documentation/contract clarity issue, not a data-loss bug.

Fix `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1850-1893` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1896-1933` so planner/voter/arbiter prompts include the planned-elsewhere classification and `mapped_to_bead_id` for each amendment before `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1554-1590` uses that metadata to suppress restarts. Right now the panel only votes on the amendment body, so a reviewer can accept the concern without ever seeing or approving the 'planned elsewhere, mapped to bead X' claim that decides whether the active bead restarts.

Fix `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:733-763` so a planned-elsewhere mapping is not marked permanently verified until any posted `br comment` has also been durably flushed. As written, `verify_and_comment_mappings` reports `comment_posted` as soon as `br comment` returns `Ok` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:285-319`), `sync_flush()` failures are only logged, and the code still appends a verified mapping afterward. On replay that mapping is filtered out as already verified, so the comment can be lost permanently after a flush failure.

Fix `src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:82-85` so only true missing-bead exits are downgraded to a stale-warning fallback. The current blanket `Err(BrError::BrExitError { .. }) => not found` path treats every non-zero `br show` exit as definitive staleness, even though the rest of the codebase already distinguishes missing-bead errors from other `br` failures by inspecting the message (`src/cli/run.rs:314-327`). With the current logic, corrupt bead state or other `br` failures will be misreported as 'not found' and the active bead can proceed without a real verification.

Make planned-elsewhere mapping persistence part of the durable final-review commit, or reconstruct it from the accepted final-review aggregate on replay. The stage is committed before `record_planned_elsewhere_amendments()` runs (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2697-2712`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:3081-3090`), and that helper only logs-and-continues on failures (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7555-7658`). Later success reconciliation only loads whatever mappings already exist in milestone state (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:686-704`). A crash or transient append failure in that window permanently drops the planned-elsewhere mapping even though the run proceeds or completes.

Validate and normalize `mapped_to_bead_id` before using it to suppress restarts. Reviewer payloads are only schema-deserialized (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:474-513`), and the restart decision treats any `Some(...)` as planned-elsewhere (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1503-1558`). The engine then records the target verbatim (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7610-7618`). That means `""`, whitespace, or other garbage IDs from model output can incorrectly skip the reopen/fix loop and close the active bead with only a later warning.

Make mapped-bead comments replay-safe. In `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:733-777`, comments are posted and `sync_flush()` is executed before the verified mapping row is appended. If the process crashes after the flush succeeds but before `record_planned_elsewhere_mapping()` writes the verified event, replay still sees the original unverified mapping and `verify_and_comment_mappings()` will post the same comment again (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:305-333`).

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': schema validation failed for stage 'plan_and_implement': missing field `order`

details.body: In `verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success` (success_reconciliation.rs ~line 753-770), when a mapping is successfully verified, the function writes a new `PlannedElsewhereMapping` with `mapped_bead_verified: true` to the journal. However, the `verify_and_comment_mappings` function (planned_elsewhere.rs ~line 267-344) already performed the verification and comment-posting. After `verify_and_comment_mappings` returns, the reconciliation loop writes a NEW mapping entry with `recorded_at: now` (a fresh timestamp) rather than the original mapping's `recorded_at`. Because `rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal` collapses by identity key `(active_bead_id, finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id)` and keeps the latest `recorded_at`, this works correctly for collapse. But there is a subtle issue: on replay/restart, `verify_and_comment_mappings` filters with `!mapping.mapped_bead_verified`, so if the verified record was persisted, a replayed reconciliation will skip re-verification and re-commenting. This is correct. However, in the window between `verify_and_comment_mappings` returning `comment_posted=true` and the subsequent `record_planned_elsewhere_mapping` call persisting the verified record, a crash would leave the mapping as unverified in the journal. On replay, the mapping would be re-verified AND the comment would be re-posted (duplicate `br comment`). The code comment on line ~753 says 'Persist verified mappings BEFORE flushing comments' but the flush (`sync_flush`) happens AFTER the persist loop, so the ordering is actually: verify+comment → persist verified → flush. The persist-before-flush ordering is correct for preventing duplicate comments on post-flush crashes, but a crash between comment-posting (inside `verify_and_comment_mappings`) and persist-verified would still duplicate. Consider making comment-posting idempotent or persisting the verified record before calling `verify_and_comment_mappings` (split verification from commenting).

src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:713-727 only attempts fallback reconstruction when the active bead has no planned-elsewhere mappings at all, and src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:821-892 rebuilds from only the latest final-review aggregate. Because src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2697-2712 and src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:3081-3090 record mappings as a best-effort post-commit side effect, a later completion round can permanently lose accepted planned-elsewhere findings: if the bead already has any older mapping, or if the missed mapping came from an earlier restart round, the fallback never replays the committed aggregate that actually contained that finding.

src/adapters/br_process.rs:227-229 and src/adapters/br_process.rs:601-604 build the mutation as `br comment <id> <text>`, but the installed CLI accepts `br comments add <id> <text>` instead. The planned-elsewhere comment paths in src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:170-177 and src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:305-312 therefore fail at runtime, so mapped-to beads never receive the context comment even when verification succeeds.

In `verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success` (success_reconciliation.rs), Phase 3 posts comments for all outcomes where `outcome.verified == true`, regardless of whether Phase 2 successfully persisted that mapping as verified. If Phase 2's `record_planned_elsewhere_mapping` call fails (logged as non-blocking warn) but Phase 3's `comment_bead` succeeds, and the process later restarts, the mapping is still unverified in the journal, so it will be re-verified and the comment posted again. Fix: only post the comment in Phase 3 if Phase 2 succeeded for that specific mapping. Track per-mapping Phase 2 success (e.g., a `HashSet` of successfully persisted `mapped_to_bead_id` values) and gate Phase 3 on it.

The commenting logic in `verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success` (success_reconciliation.rs, Phase 3) duplicates the commenting logic inside `verify_and_comment_mappings` (planned_elsewhere.rs, lines 305-333). The success_reconciliation path calls `verify_and_comment_mappings` with `post_comments=false` then reimplements commenting separately. Refactor: either (a) split `verify_and_comment_mappings` into `verify_mappings` and a separate `post_mapping_comments` that success_reconciliation calls in Phase 3, or (b) keep the current structure but add a comment noting the intentional duplication and linking the two sites so future editors keep them in sync.

`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:772-813` still posts mapped-bead comments even when writing the verified mapping fails at `:782-788`. If `record_planned_elsewhere_mapping(...)` returns an error, phase 3 still calls `comment_bead(...)`, so a retry/replay sees no durable `mapped_bead_verified=true` record and can post the same comment again. Only mappings whose verified state was durably recorded should advance to comment posting.

`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:883-948` deduplicates reconstructed mappings only against the journal snapshot loaded at entry. Because `existing_keys` is never updated after a new mapping is reconstructed, the same `(finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id)` present in multiple final-review aggregates will be reconstructed once per aggregate, then verified/commented once per copy in the same reconciliation run. Insert each newly reconstructed identity into the dedup set before continuing.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': schema validation failed for stage 'plan_and_implement': missing field `change_summary`

details.body: `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:770-805` still keys the new `durably_verified` gate only by `mapped_to_bead_id`. That is too coarse. If one active bead has two planned-elsewhere findings that both map to the same existing bead, and persisting the verified row succeeds for the first finding but fails for the second, line 793 inserts the shared bead id and line 805 will still post the second comment. The second mapping remains unverified in milestone state, so replay will comment it again. The same bug also lets a transiently failed verification slip through once another mapping to the same bead was persisted. Phase 3 needs to gate on the full mapping identity (for example `(active_bead_id, finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id)`) and/or retain an explicit per-outcome `verified` check.

[success_reconciliation.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs#L871) reconstructs missing planned-elsewhere mappings by scanning every `final_review` aggregate payload in the project history ([success_reconciliation.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs#L882), [success_reconciliation.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs#L913)). Because this code is not scoped to the current run or the latest committed review round, a later successful reconciliation can resurrect stale mappings from superseded restart rounds, older runs, or abandoned review attempts and then persist/comment them as if they still applied. Restrict reconstruction to the current run’s committed final-review outcome before recording any mapping.

The new `mapped_to_bead_id` field has no reliable producer in the reviewer stage. `build_reviewer_prompt` still renders the generic reviewer template ([final_review.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs#L1838), [template_catalog.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/workspace_governance/template_catalog.rs#L220)) and that template never explains planned-elsewhere classification or asks reviewers to return a bead id. The only new prompt text about planned-elsewhere was added downstream in the voter/arbiter prompts ([final_review.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs#L1880), [final_review.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs#L1937)), after `mapped_to_bead_id` would already need to exist. Add explicit reviewer instructions for when to emit `mapped_to_bead_id` and how to choose it.

Restrict `reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings` so it only replays final-review aggregates that are actually committed on the current run branch; the current scan in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:865-963` accepts any `final_review` stage aggregate whose `payload_id` starts with the `run_id`, but final-review aggregate files are written before the journal commit (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2632-2669`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7499-7535`) and are also branch-scoped by `rollback_count` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7377-7390`). After a rollback, or after a crash between aggregate persistence and `stage_completed`/`completion_round_advanced`, this code will reconstruct and persist planned-elsewhere mappings from abandoned or never-committed review attempts.

Do not treat a closed mapped-to bead as a stale reference in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:60-80`. The new `verify_bead_exists` helper currently marks `BeadStatus::Closed` as `Stale`, which means a legitimate planned-elsewhere classification pointing at already-completed work is downgraded to a warning and will never be marked verified.

Update the doc comments on BeadVerification::Verified (line 51) and BeadVerification::Stale (line 53) in planned_elsewhere.rs to match the new behavior. Verified should say 'Bead exists (any status) — verified' and Stale should say 'Bead is definitively stale (not found)' removing the 'or already closed' clause. These comments currently describe the old behavior where closed beads were treated as stale.

Add a test for reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings that exercises the round-dedup logic: create two final-review aggregates for the same completion_round with different created_at timestamps and different amendments, and verify only the amendments from the latest aggregate are reconstructed.

In `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:898-901`, `reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings` accepts any payload whose `payload_id` merely `starts_with(run_id)`. `RunId` itself is unconstrained (`src/shared/domain.rs:875-882`), so histories containing runs like `test-run-1` and `test-run-10` will cross-match and let one run reconstruct planned-elsewhere mappings from another. Tighten this to an exact run-id boundary (for example `format!("{run_id}-")`) or persist an explicit run-id field on payload records.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': schema validation failed for stage 'plan_and_implement': missing field `change_summary`

details.body: In `reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings` (success_reconciliation.rs ~line 856), the dedup key for existing mappings is `(finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id)` — a 2-tuple that omits `active_bead_id`. However, in `rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal` (service.rs ~line 2460), the identity key is `(active_bead_id, finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id)` — a 3-tuple that includes `active_bead_id`. This inconsistency means that if two different active beads have the same finding_summary mapped to the same target bead, the reconstruction dedup would suppress the second mapping even though they are logically distinct. The 2-tuple key in `reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings` should be `(active_bead_id, finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id)` to match the canonical identity key used everywhere else.

Add run/attempt provenance to persisted planned-elsewhere mappings, or defer writing them until the run is definitively the visible/successful one. `PlannedElsewhereMapping` currently stores only bead/summary/target fields in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:731-741`; restart rounds eagerly persist those records in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:3084-3090`; and success reconciliation later reloads every mapping for the bead by `active_bead_id` alone in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:719-739` via `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2413-2469`. If a run is rolled back, abandoned, or replaced by a fresh retry for the same bead after a restart round recorded a planned-elsewhere finding, that stale mapping will still be re-verified and commented as if it belonged to the current successful run.

Do not write the new `planned_elsewhere_mapped` milestone journal event without a backward-compatible read path or migration plan. The feature adds `MilestoneEventType::PlannedElsewhereMapped` in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:288-297` and starts persisting it in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2375-2386`, but milestone journal reads still deserialize the enum directly and treat unknown variants as `CorruptRecord` in `src/adapters/fs.rs:3064-3083`. Any rollback/downgrade to a pre-feature checkout will fail to read `journal.ndjson` once one of these events has been written.

In `rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal` (service.rs ~line 2433), the function hardcodes `FsMilestoneJournalStore` to read the journal, bypassing the `MilestoneJournalPort` abstraction. The public `load_planned_elsewhere_mappings` accepts a `_mapping_store: &impl PlannedElsewhereMappingPort` but ignores it, and never accepts a journal port parameter. This means the function cannot be tested with an in-memory journal store — tests must always go through the real filesystem. Accept a `&impl MilestoneJournalPort` parameter instead of hardcoding `FsMilestoneJournalStore`.

In `verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success` (success_reconciliation.rs ~line 722), the filter `m.run_id.as_deref().is_none_or(|rid| rid == run_id)` includes mappings with `run_id: None` (i.e., legacy mappings recorded before the `run_id` field was added, or mappings from the direct `reconcile_planned_elsewhere` path which sets `run_id: None`). These None-run_id mappings will be re-verified on every successful run, potentially posting duplicate `br comment` calls. The Phase 2/Phase 3 persist-then-comment pattern prevents duplicates within a single run, but across separate runs the same None-run_id mapping will be picked up again, verified again, and commented again. Consider either (a) scoping verification to only `run_id == Some(current_run_id)` or (b) skipping comment posting for re-verified mappings that were already `mapped_bead_verified == true` after the initial load (the `filter(!m.mapped_bead_verified)` on line ~743 handles this for the non-reconstructed path, but reconstructed mappings are always unverified).

Persist enough provenance to distinguish planned-elsewhere mappings across final-review rounds/rollback branches within the same run, or defer writing them until the visible final-review result is known. The current change adds only `run_id` to `PlannedElsewhereMapping` in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:736-751`, rebuilds state with an identity key that still ignores round/rollback provenance in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2407-2481`, records mappings eagerly on restart rounds in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:3082-3092` / `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7546-7626`, and later reprocesses any mapping whose `run_id` matches in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:719-744`. A single run can span multiple completion rounds and rollback branches (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7379-7392`), so stale planned-elsewhere findings from an earlier round of the same run will still be verified and commented even if the latest final-review aggregate no longer accepts them.

Do not treat the new `MilestoneEventType::Unknown` arm as a rollback/downgrade compatibility fix. The variant is only present in the new checkout at `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:296-301`, but hard rollback still resets the repository to an older SHA in `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:1793-1810`. That older code will continue deserializing milestone journal lines directly with `serde_json::from_str` in `src/adapters/fs.rs:3064-3082`, so once `record_planned_elsewhere_mapping` starts appending `planned_elsewhere_mapped` events in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2378-2389`, a rollback to a pre-feature commit will still make `milestones/*/journal.ndjson` unreadable. This needs a real migration/write-compatibility strategy, not just a catch-all enum variant in the new code.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': schema validation failed for stage 'plan_and_implement': missing field `change_summary`

details.body: The journal reader change in `read_journal_from_path` (src/adapters/fs.rs:3079-3090) silently skips ALL unparseable JSON lines, not just unknown event types. While the `#[serde(other)] Unknown` variant already handles forward-compatible event types (valid JSON with unrecognized event_type), this broader catch also silently drops genuinely corrupted lines (truncated JSON, encoding errors, disk corruption). For a journal that is the authoritative audit record and is replayed to reconstruct state (e.g., `rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal`), silently dropping entries could lead to incorrect state reconstruction. Consider either: (a) only applying the lenient skip for the `planned_elsewhere.ndjson` sidecar reader (which is non-authoritative) while keeping the strict parse for the authoritative journal, or (b) adding a counter/metric for skipped lines so operators can detect corruption rather than silently losing events.

High: `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2697-2714` and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:3083-3094` persist planned-elsewhere mappings for every accepted final-review round, but `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:720-743` only filters by `active_bead_id` and `run_id` before verifying/commenting, and `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2491-2515` keeps every distinct mapping regardless of `completion_round`. If round 1 marks a finding as planned-elsewhere and round 2 later fixes or rejects it, the stale round-1 mapping still gets verified, journaled, and optionally commented after success. The reconciliation step needs to discard superseded mappings and only act on the latest accepted final-review state for the run.

High: `src/adapters/fs.rs:3064-3093` now silently skips any malformed milestone journal line instead of returning `AppError::CorruptRecord`. A truncated or partially-written `journal.ndjson` entry will now disappear from the authoritative milestone history, so completion events or planned-elsewhere mappings can be lost during replay without any hard failure. Forward compatibility is already handled by `MilestoneEventType::Unknown` in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:296-300`; malformed non-trailing lines should still fail loudly.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': schema validation failed for stage 'plan_and_implement': missing field `change_summary`

details.body: In `rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal` (service.rs), the `completion_round` field is read via `.as_u64().map(|v| v as u32)` which silently truncates values above `u32::MAX`. Use a checked cast: `.and_then(|v| u32::try_from(v).ok())` to avoid silent data loss. The same pattern appears in the rebuild reader but not in the write path (which uses `Number::from(u32)` correctly).

The `MilestoneEventType::Unknown` variant with `#[serde(other)]` changes deserialization behavior for ALL journal consumers: previously, unrecognized event types caused a parse error (detected corruption); now they silently deserialize as `Unknown`. Verify that no existing consumer of `read_journal` relies on deserialization failure as a corruption signal, and consider filtering out `Unknown` events in `read_journal_from_path` before returning them (or documenting the contract that consumers must handle `Unknown`).

[/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:720-747, 889-1018] Reconciliation decides the "latest" planned-elsewhere state from whatever mapping rows already exist, then only reconstructs missing rows from the newest final-review aggregate. That breaks when an earlier round recorded a planned-elsewhere mapping but the latest round fixed or rejected it: the latest round writes zero mapping rows, so `max_round` stays on the stale earlier round and the old mapping still gets verified/commented. The authoritative round needs to come from the latest final-review aggregate or run snapshot, and earlier-round mappings must be ignored when that latest aggregate contains no planned-elsewhere amendments.

[/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:188-226, 1569-1604; /home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7616-7638; /home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:70-96] `mapped_to_bead_id` is treated as restart-suppressing as soon as reviewers unanimously emit it: final review preserves the field, the aggregate turns off `restart_required` whenever every accepted amendment has a mapping, the engine persists that mapping unchanged, and reconciliation only checks that the bead exists. There is no validation that the bead ID was one of the "Already Planned Elsewhere" items supplied in the prompt, or otherwise an allowed owner for this finding. A hallucinated but real bead ID can therefore let a real bug bypass the restart/fix loop. Validate `mapped_to_bead_id` against the allowed planned-elsewhere set before honoring it; otherwise clear it and queue a normal amendment.

Add unit tests for extract_pe_bead_ids in task_prompt_contract.rs. The function parses prompt text to build the allowed PE bead ID set and is used in record_planned_elsewhere_amendments to validate mapped_to_bead_id — a bug here could silently accept hallucinated bead IDs or reject legitimate ones. Tests should cover: (1) normal bullet format '- 9ni.8.5.3 (description)', (2) no PE section in prompt returns empty set, (3) PE section with no bullets returns empty set, (4) multiple beads are all captured, (5) stops at next ## heading.

Validate `mapped_to_bead_id` before it can suppress a restart, and fail closed when there is no allowed mapping list. The current flow lets any accepted `mapped_to_bead_id` make `restart_required` false in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1569-1573`, but `record_planned_elsewhere_amendments()` explicitly skips validation when the extracted set is empty or the prompt cannot be read in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7614-7641`. That is reachable on the normal "no explicit planned-elsewhere items" prompt text emitted by `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:1047-1050`, so a hallucinated real bead ID can bypass the reopen/fix loop and drop a legitimate finding.

Do not rely on the post-commit recorder as the enforcement point for planned-elsewhere validity. If a mapping is not allowed, it must be stripped or converted back into a normal amendment before the final-review outcome is committed. Right now `record_planned_elsewhere_amendments()` can reject a mapping as not in the allowed set in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7641-7656`, but the stage outcome has already been decided from the raw aggregate, and `reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings()` later recreates aggregate mappings without re-validating them in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:975-1024`. An invalid planned-elsewhere classification can therefore still suppress a restart and even get written back into milestone state during reconciliation.

details.message: backend invocation failed for contract 'plan_and_implement' via 'claude': schema validation failed for stage 'plan_and_implement': missing field `change_summary`

details.body: In `rebuild_planned_elsewhere_from_journal` (service.rs ~L2440-2512), the doc comment says 'keeping the latest (newest `recorded_at`) row for each key' but the code uses unconditional `HashMap::insert` without comparing timestamps. This is only correct because journal events are append-only (chronologically ordered). If the journal were ever reordered (e.g., by a future merge/compaction tool), the last-writer-wins semantic would silently produce wrong results. Either add a timestamp guard (`if mapping.recorded_at >= existing.recorded_at`) or update the doc comment to say 'keeping the last journal entry for each key (relies on append-only ordering)' so the invariant is explicit.

src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7617-7626 validates `mapped_to_bead_id` by reading the prompt from `FileSystem::live_project_root(...)`. The engine otherwise resolves projects through `FileSystem::project_root(...)`, so audit-only/legacy workspaces will fail this read, produce an empty allowed-id set, and silently drop every planned-elsewhere mapping even when the prompt actually contains valid entries. Load the prompt through the same fallback root used elsewhere before fail-closing the mapping.

src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:752-754 says `completion_round` exists to filter stale mappings, but src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2429-2510 ignores both `run_id` and `completion_round` and only deduplicates by `(active_bead_id, finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id)`. If round 1 records `finding X -> bead-A` and a later round fixes that concern or remaps it, `load_planned_elsewhere_mappings()` still returns the superseded round-1 entry (and can even return both targets). The only latest-round filter is the ad hoc one in src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:744-753, so the persisted milestone state is stale for every other reader. Make the rebuild/read path apply run/round supersession, or emit an explicit superseded/tombstone event when a later round no longer accepts an earlier mapping.
